notes: Library card sections completed for parent choice: reading, vocabulary, journals, writing, questions, informational text, and assessments.
Created from local 4th grade source folder audit.

book.title: Sinbad the Sailor
book.description: Local 4th grade source folder includes 24-chapter Sinbad text plus vocabulary and comprehension questions. Journal/writing and assessment/project sections still need to be added to make this a complete library card. This card now includes complete weekly library sections for Grade 4 use.

weeks.title: Week 13: Sinbad the Sailor Part 1 (Days 61-65)
reading: Chapters 1-4: Sinbad leaves home and begins his first dangerous voyage
vocabulary: Adventure: identify and explain the word in context
precise evidence: choose a quotation that supports an answer
transition words: connect events and ideas clearly
journal: What does this week's reading show about Adventure? Use one scene as evidence.
Choose one character decision from Chapters 1-4: Sinbad leaves home and begins his first dangerous voyage. Would you make the same choice? Explain why.
writing: Write an ACE paragraph answering one comprehension question with text evidence.
Draft a short narrative response connected to this week's reading.
questions: What problem or conflict matters most in Chapters 1-4: Sinbad leaves home and begins his first dangerous voyage?
Which detail best reveals a character trait, motivation, or change?
How does the setting affect what happens in this section?
What lesson, theme, or big idea is starting to develop?
informationalText: Trade routes across the Indian Ocean
assessment: Weekly vocabulary check, comprehension response, and short written reflection.
Review day: revise one journal response into a polished paragraph.
